last_item: Backups + Settings co-location — THE canonical seed item (4 of 11 audit agents): backup config (S3 destination, retention, encryption) lived on /settings while backup operations (run, history, restore) lived on /backups; configure-then-run meant bouncing routes (high/M).
last_result: SHIPPED. Co-located by MOVING (not duplicating): extracted the config form into shared web/src/components/BackupStorageForm.tsx (identical fields + save-doubles-as-connection-test) and surfaced it ON /backups via a 'Backup storage' header button → right-side Sheet. The LocalBackupWarning CTA ('Set up an S3 bucket') now opens that same Sheet in-page instead of <Link to=/settings>, so the user never leaves the backups workflow; Sheet stays open after save so the connection-test result persists. Removed the form from Settings → now just Database tuning + Connection pooler + a one-line info Callout pointing to /backups (so muscle-memory users aren't stranded). Single home, no duplication, Settings simpler (28 lines). Tests: moved the 4 form tests → BackupStorageForm.test.tsx; rewrote Settings.test (form gone + pointer link to /backups); updated Backups LocalBackupWarning test (link → button calling onConfigure). 140 tests. Panel: Sam REJECTED first (on a FAILED Save&connect a non-expert can't tell if they're still protected; green 'Stored in S3' badge could imply working off-host backups over a broken target). Verified real behavior in internal/server/handlers_config.go (config saves + live target switches even when stanza-create fails → new backups to it fail until fixed; past/local backups untouched), then led the failed-save Callout with an HONEST reassurance ('Your existing backups are untouched and still safe — but new backups to this bucket will fail until this is fixed…') rather than the false 'still lands locally'. Sam's badge point is backend-limited (GET /config has no target-health signal) → out of frontend-loop scope, filed as a NEEDS-BACKEND backlog item. Re-ran Sam → SHIP. Also fixed two cheap nits: stale success copy 'run a backup from the Backups page' → 'Close this panel and run a backup' (you're already there), and a stale backupDestination doc comment ('The Settings form' → 'The backup-storage form'). Panel: 4 SHIP, zero blockers (UX heuristics — 'directly fixes the panel's own most-cited UX problem'; Sam — after the honest fix; Priya — 'one click from where I run backups, fewer route hops than before, nothing buried'; restraint critic — 'kills a top-cited route bounce at net-neutral surface; form moved not duplicated; Settings got simpler'). Gates: typecheck OK, 140 tests OK, build OK (dist regenerated + staged), go build OK (outside sandbox — snap-confine blocks in-sandbox). Next top item: Migrate — reset form state when returning from a terminal job ('Start another' leaves source/target/overwrite pre-filled) (med/M).
notes: PURE UX-IMPROVEMENT LOOP. Sibling of the COMPLETED scripts/ralph/ shadcn-migration track. Capability parity is sacred (Vitest suite is the contract); layout/flow/copy are free to change. Restraint over churn — rejecting a change and recording why is a normal outcome. Frontend/UX only, never touch Go. go build is blocked in-sandbox (snap-confine); run with sandbox disabled. See PROMPT.md + UX-PRINCIPLES.md + PERSONAS.md + UI-RULES.md.
north_star: The indiepg panel is the easiest, clearest, safest way for an indie hacker to run their Postgres — minimal surface, co-located workflows, honest state, no over-design. Stop when the UX has converged (stable_streak >= 3).
